description: Imported GitHub issue #453 after deciding that maintainer tooling should be an opt-in pack rather than a special yzx dev mode. Add a top-level maintainer pack for repo maintenance tools that are meaningfully tied to maintaining Yazelix itself, starting with gh, prek, tru, beads-rust, and beads-viewer. Keep runtime behavior unchanged, sync the default config and Home Manager module, document the pack in the collection/docs, and add a focused regression test that verifies the pack stays aligned across configuration paths.
close_reason: Added an opt-in maintainer pack with gh, prek, tru, beads-rust, and beads-viewer; synced the default config, Home Manager module, and collection docs; and added a regression test that keeps the pack aligned across both configuration paths.

title: Fix missing Yazelix plugin permission popup on fresh Zellij startup
description: On fresh Yazelix launches under Zellij 0.44, the pane orchestrator/popup runner permission popup may never appear even after clearing Zellij runtime/cache/share state. Only zjstatus gets a persisted permission block, while Yazelix plugins remain ungranted until permissions are seeded manually in permissions.kdl.
notes: Added repo-level recovery path on 2026-03-27: `yzx repair zellij-permissions` now seeds the tracked/runtime permission blocks for the pane orchestrator and popup runner into ~/.cache/zellij/permissions.kdl and tells the user to restart Yazelix. This is an explicit recovery/workaround path, not the root-cause fix for the missing popup.
close_reason: Superseded by the startup-handoff hardening and permission recovery work on 2026-03-27. The missing Yazelix plugin permission popup was a downstream symptom of the broken Zellij startup path; commit 5043aeb removed the boot-time plugin IPC and duplicate launch-tree hazard, and commit 142e4c8 added an explicit recovery path for stale permission state.

title: Ghostty trail glow "none" still renders extreme glow
description:
Imported GitHub issue #466.

### Details

When glow is set to `none`, Yazelix still renders a very large spatial glow instead of disabling the effect.
close_reason: Completed: fixed Ghostty cursor glow mask math and normalized trail glow behavior so none/low/medium/high behave correctly across variants.

title: Evaluate Pretext-inspired text-first welcome screen
description: Use the Pretext site as inspiration for another strong Yazelix welcome-screen direction, focused on text-first composition rather than only boxed logo cards.
design:
Use the Pretext site as inspiration for a more text-forward Yazelix welcome treatment, but test the palette and copy hierarchy step by step instead of composing multiple experimental color systems at once.

Color findings from the shipped logo asset (assets/logo.png): the brand palette is red, green, yellow, blue, and purple. In practice, purple and red should stay out of readable body copy; they work better as wordmark or border accents. The next controlled trials should compare:
- Brand Calm: blue body base with yellow highlights and green only for special emphasis like SSH
- Matrix Clean: green body base with restrained yellow highlights

Preferred first trial: Matrix Clean, because it fits the terminal-native identity and the user explicitly likes that direction. Keep the wordmark multicolor, keep the border single-color, and keep the body text color system simple: one base color plus one accent color. Avoid alternating full-line palettes and avoid more than one or two highlighted terms per line.

This bead is specifically about the text card composition and palette, not about adding another animation engine.
acceptance_criteria: There is a documented, repo-backed color direction for the text-first welcome card that is derived from the shipped logo rather than ad hoc terminal guesses. At least one restrained palette variant is tested end to end on the real welcome card, with width-fit checks, readability checks on the actual background, and a clear keep/change decision before moving on to more experimentation. The implementation should preserve the existing welcome_style system and should not add extra color complexity unless a previous simpler variant has been evaluated and rejected.

title: Auto-apply safe config migrations at entrypoints while keeping yzx config migrate
description:
Imported GitHub issue #478.

Yazelix should automatically apply deterministic config migrations when the user enters the product through the normal interactive entrypoints, instead of forcing them to manually run `yzx config migrate --apply` for every stale-but-safe case. This should make startup, launch, restart, and interactive `yzx env` feel self-healing for safe rewrites while preserving explicit control and clear diagnostics for ambiguous cases.

Do not remove `yzx config migrate`. It should remain the canonical preview/apply/debug surface for migrations. The change here is to make the common entrypoints invoke the same safe migration engine automatically when that is clearly safe.
design:
Reason from first principles:
- Deterministic rewrites with backups are product-maintenance work, not user work, so Yazelix should usually handle them automatically when the user is trying to launch or enter the environment.
- Read paths must stay read-only. Do not hide file mutations inside `parse_yazelix_config` or other helpers used by widgets, config generation, or general state inspection. Ordinary reads should not rewrite user config.
- Keep the existing split between safe `auto` migrations and `manual_only` migrations. Automatic entrypoint behavior must only apply the `auto` subset and must leave manual-only items explicit.

Scope:
- Add a migration preflight for interactive entrypoints such as `yzx launch`, `yzx restart`, normal Yazelix startup, and interactive `yzx env`.
- If the migration plan has only deterministic auto rewrites, apply them automatically with backups, then continue into the normal entrypoint flow and print a short high-signal notice.
- If the plan has both auto and manual-only items, apply only the auto rewrites, then continue while printing the remaining manual follow-up clearly.
- If the situation is ambiguous or ownership-sensitive, keep failing fast with the current actionable diagnostics instead of guessing.
- In non-interactive or read-only contexts, do not mutate automatically.

Non-goals:
- Do not remove `yzx config migrate`.
- Do not make `parse_yazelix_config` mutate files.
- Do not auto-apply `manual_only` migrations.
- Do not silently resolve duplicate or conflicting ownership cases that still require user intent.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Interactive entrypoints (`yzx launch`, `yzx restart`, normal Yazelix startup, and interactive `yzx env`) run a migration preflight before normal config consumption.
2. When the migration plan contains only `auto` changes, Yazelix applies them automatically with backup and continues into the entrypoint flow without requiring the user to run `yzx config migrate --apply` manually.
3. When the plan contains both `auto` and `manual_only` items, Yazelix applies only the `auto` subset, surfaces the remaining manual items clearly, and still fails fast when those manual-only items require user intent before startup can continue.
4. When the config state is ambiguous or ownership-sensitive, Yazelix still fails fast with clear guidance instead of auto-mutating.
5. Non-interactive/read-only paths such as widget rendering, general config parsing helpers, and other routine readers do not mutate config files.
6. `yzx config migrate` remains available as the explicit preview/apply/debug surface and still reports the same plan semantics.
7. Add focused regression coverage for: auto-apply on at least one interactive entrypoint, mixed auto/manual plans, non-interactive no-mutation behavior, and continued fail-fast handling for ambiguous ownership conflicts.
close_reason: Completed: interactive entrypoints now auto-apply deterministic config migrations with backups, still fail fast on remaining manual-only follow-up, and keep parse/read paths non-mutating.

title: Re-verify Alt y does not re-enable pane frames zellij-wide
description: GitHub issue #484 reports that pressing Alt y makes pane frames appear again across the Zellij session and reintroduces the shaky pane-frame bug. Current code history includes a fix removing the old frame-toggle hook, but this needs live behavioral verification before the issue can be closed.
close_reason: Fixed the Alt+y pane-frame regression; the remaining sidebar-reopen bug is tracked separately.
